scope: isolated repository execution implementation and development-provider evidence through RF-8312

redGreenFindings.red: Vercel network header transformation was unavailable on the Hobby plan.
redGreenFindings.green: Trusted-host bounded download now injects bytes into an always-deny-all sandbox without forwarding authorization to the temporary archive URL.

redGreenFindings.red: A repeated trusted supervisor mkdir failed when the directory already existed.
redGreenFindings.green: Each experiment now receives a unique supervisor directory and the repeated live gate passes.

redGreenFindings.red: Five nominal 1 ms reduced-motion React effects exceeded a one-second browser assertion under concurrent load.
redGreenFindings.green: Reduced-motion activation now skips staged animation in one state transition; five focused repetitions and the complete 18-test browser suite pass.

redGreenFindings.red: The heaviest 500-case adversarial property exceeded Vitest's generic 5-second per-test timeout under aggregate load without an assertion failure.
redGreenFindings.green: The unchanged 500-case property declares a bounded 30-second harness timeout and the complete four-property file passes three consecutive focused runs.

redGreenFindings.red: Four unrelated Playwright journeys reached the shared test timeout while competing for on-demand Next development compilation after the production build.
redGreenFindings.green: The release browser gate starts the already-built production server on dedicated port 3129; all 18 journeys pass in 15.5 seconds without weakening assertions or timeouts.

redGreenFindings.red: The linked Hobby preview rejected the Queue Function's 900-second maxDuration as outside the plan limit.
redGreenFindings.green: A deployment-contract test pins both the Next route and vercel.json to the universally Hobby-compatible 60-second ceiling; longer hosted orchestration remains an explicit 8D gate.

visualEvidence.reason: This backend boundary is materially verified by provider behavior, source hash identity, network denial, bounded output, cancellation, fresh restores, cleanup, and the portable machine-readable bundle. Hosted browser and ChatGPT visual requirements remain in 8D/9.

architectureDiagram.alt: ReproForge trust architecture showing the trusted sample and authorized immutable GitHub source entering deterministic proof through a disposable deny-all sandbox.
architectureDiagram.caption: Current trust boundary: proposals remain non-executing, while a provider-verified sandbox path supplies evidence to deterministic verification and portable export.
architectureDiagram.provenance: First-party ReproForge SVG rendered locally with Playwright Chromium and visually inspected; no external product capture, private data, or generated imagery.

sanitization: No credentials, provider resource IDs, session IDs, database identifiers, object keys, provider URLs, response headers, private source content, customer data, or personal data are committed.
